attack_surface.data_description: Governance proposal submission originating from an address claiming a false identity

security_gap.gap_description: The on-chain governance contract does not verify the real-world or protocol identity associated with the submitting address, only the minimum $BGT requirement.

actor_risk.description: The actor can submit proposals and may attempt to deceive the community about their true identity.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Spoofing identity allows deceiving the community about the source of a proposal, potentially influencing voting, but doesn't directly grant on-chain power without further governance steps.

assumptions: Actor 3 can acquire an address meeting the 10k $BGT threshold.
The governance contract relies solely on msg.sender and balance for submission, not external identity proof.
The community or system relies on the claimed identity during the RFRV review process.

mitigation.strategy: Strengthen off-chain verification steps for the RFRV process, potentially linking on-chain addresses to verified identities.

description: A partner protocol (or other entity meeting the $BGT threshold) may submit a governance proposal claiming to represent a different, potentially more reputable, protocol or entity to gain community trust or deceive voters during the governance process.
